reasoning: The original 30-day state of emergency was declared for an electricity crisis on March 31, 2025, and is scheduled to expire at the end of April 29, 2025. On April 21, 2025, Samoa's Electric Power Corporation officially cancelled all remaining power rationing because 5 large rental generators had been brought online, fully meeting the island's demand for electricity for the next 6 months. With the acute phase of the crisis resolved well before the scheduled expiration date, the most likely outcome by far is that the government will simply allow the state of emergency to lapse on April 29, 2025, without extending it into April 30. Checking current news confirms no other valid states of emergency or announced extensions.
